questions.text: What is the supreme law of the Federal Republic of Nigeria?
questions.explanation: The Constitution of Nigeria is supreme, and its provisions have binding force on all authorities and persons throughout the country.

questions.text: According to the Nigerian Constitution, a person can acquire citizenship by birth if:
options: They reside in Nigeria for 15 years
Either of their parents or grandparents was born in Nigeria and belongs to a community indigenous to Nigeria
They are married to a citizen of Nigeria
They register with the local government authority
questions.explanation: Citizenship by birth under Section 25 of the 1999 Constitution is granted to anyone born in or outside Nigeria whose parents/grandparents are citizens of Nigeria by birth.

questions.text: Which organ of government is primarily responsible for making laws in Nigeria?
questions.explanation: The Legislature (the National Assembly, consisting of the Senate and House of Representatives) is responsible for law-making.

questions.text: The arm of government responsible for interpreting laws and resolving disputes is the:
questions.explanation: The Judiciary (the court system headed by the Chief Justice of Nigeria) interprets the laws and administers justice.

questions.text: Which of the following is a fundamental civic duty of a Nigerian citizen?
options: Voting in elections, paying taxes, and respecting national symbols
questions.explanation: Civic duties are obligations of citizens, which include law obedience, tax payment, voting, and national respect.
